string.text: Memory multiplier. (If NPC already saw target then NPC will receive boost to their vision against this target. Decreases with time, with default value of 1.0 it takes around 100 real-life seconds for NPC to drop their boost to 0 against this target) \n \n Default: 1.0
Distance multiplier. (Distance from object to object, range and fov) \n \n Default: 1.0
Recommended to enable this if you use Michiko's weather.

Displaying equation variables (for correct threshold you have to keep cursor on object all the time): 
 
 Console: all variables 
 
 Dynamic news: if object see actor or not 
 
 Bottom of the screen message: visibility threshold of object (and variables that you can change in MCM)
Enable the Combat AI Aim system, which dynamically adjusts NPC aim angles, speed, and prediction based on conditions, weather, and NPC rank. \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable the alternative Danger Alert system for NPCs. This system allows NPCs to react dynamically to hostile sounds and attacks, providing enhanced combat awareness. \n \n Default: Enabled

Enable the NPC detection awareness in pre-combat. This system controls how NPCs perceive the player before entering active combat, based on movement rank, distance, speed, weight carried, time of day, weather, and light sources. Enabling this ensures NPCs respond appropriately to the player while still in pre-combat situations, adjusting their awareness according to rank and environmental conditions. Disabling it reverts NPCs to vanilla detection behavior. This does not affect combat behavior once the player is detected; it only governs awareness prior to entering combat. \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable rank-based modifiers for NPC detection checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much NPC rank affects detection checks. \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable weather-based modifiers for NPC detection checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much weather affects detection checks. \n \n Default: 1.0

Enable the NPC sound awareness in pre-combat. This system governs how NPCs perceive sounds before entering active combat, taking into account rank, distance, environmental conditions, and weather. Enabling this ensures NPCs react appropriately to sound while still in pre-combat situations. Disabling it reverts NPCs to vanilla sound detection behavior. The vanilla system, NPCs have limited sound perception during active combat. Sound sensitivity during combat is controlled separately via the "In Combat Sound Limit" slider. \n \n Default: Enabled
Multiplier for NPC reaction to danger sounds (Weapon shoot\hit. Monster hit\attack). \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to weapon sounds (Weapon empty\reload\swith weapons.) \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to mutant sounds (Monster steps\talk\eat.). \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to item-related sounds (Item use\pickup\drop). \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable NPCs detect sounds in combat \n \n Default: Enabled

Adjusts the minimum\maximum sound range NPCs can detect while in combat. Higher values make NPCs more sensitive to sound during fights, while lower values reduce their auditory range. \n \n Default: 35.0
Enable rank-based modifiers for NPC sound checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much NPC rank affects sound checks. \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable weather-based modifiers for NPC sound detection. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much weather affects NPC sound detection. \n \n Default: 1.0
